Function Reference

taxodist
Get Started Statistical Applications Reference GitHub ↗
Reference

Function Reference

Complete API reference for taxodist in R and Python. All functions are available in both languages with equivalent signatures.

Distance
taxo_distance() distance_matrix() focal_distances() closest_relative() mrca()
Lineage
get_lineage() get_lineage_by_id() get_taxonomicon_id() taxo_search() compare_lineages() shared_clades() taxo_path() lineage_depth() is_member() filter_clade() check_coverage()
Multivariate
taxo_cluster() taxo_ordinate() taxo_heatmap()
Cache
clear_cache() save_cache() load_cache() cache_info()
Distance computation
taxo_distance() taxo_distance(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Compute the taxonomic distance between two taxa. Retrieves both lineages from The Taxonomicon, finds the most recent common ancestor, and returns 1 / depth(MRCA). Also returns the MRCA name, its depth, and the individual lineage depths.

→ named list / dict: distance, mrca, mrca_depth, depth_a, depth_b
distance_matrix() distance_matrix(taxa)

Compute the full symmetric pairwise distance matrix for a vector of taxon names. All lineages are cached after retrieval. Returns a standard dist object (R) or a scipy-compatible distance matrix (Python) with zeros on the diagonal. Taxa not found in The Taxonomicon produce NA distances.

→ dist object (R) / ndarray (Python)
focal_distances() focal_distances(focal, community)

Compute distances from a single focal taxon to every member of a community vector. Equivalent to a single row of distance_matrix() but more efficient when only one focal taxon is needed.

→ named numeric vector / Series
closest_relative() closest_relative(query, candidates)

Find the closest relative of a query taxon among a set of candidates. Returns all candidates ranked by distance (ascending). Useful for nearest-neighbour classification and phylogenetic placement.

→ data.frame / DataFrame: taxon, distance (sorted)
mrca() mrca(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Return the name of the most recent common ancestor of two taxa as recorded in The Taxonomicon hierarchy.

→ character string / str
Lineage utilities
get_lineage() get_lineage(taxon)

Retrieve the full hierarchical lineage of a taxon by name, from the root (Biota) down to the taxon itself. Accepts names at any rank: species, genus, family, order, or any named clade. Results are cached in memory.

→ character vector / list of strings
get_lineage_by_id() get_lineage_by_id(id)

Retrieve a lineage using The Taxonomicon's numeric ID instead of a name. Useful for disambiguating homonyms — use taxo_search() or the Search Database tab in the web app to find the correct ID.

→ character vector / list of strings
get_taxonomicon_id() get_taxonomicon_id(taxon)

Resolve a taxon name to its numeric Taxonomicon ID. Returns the ID of the first match. For homonyms, use taxo_search() to inspect all matches before committing to an ID.

→ integer / int
taxo_search() taxo_search(query)

Search The Taxonomicon for all taxa matching a query string. Returns a table of matches with their numeric IDs, accepted names, and ranks — essential for resolving homonyms or finding the correct entry for a historical classification.

→ data.frame / DataFrame: id, name, rank, accepted_name
compare_lineages() compare_lineages(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Print a side-by-side comparison of two lineages, showing the shared trunk up to the MRCA and the unique portion of each lineage below the split.

→ invisibly returns a list: shared, unique_a, unique_b
shared_clades() shared_clades(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Return the shared portion of the lineage — all nodes from the root down to and including the MRCA.

→ character vector / list of strings
taxo_path() taxo_path(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Return the node-by-node path from taxon A up to the MRCA and then down to taxon B — the shortest path through the tree connecting the two taxa.

→ character vector / list of strings
lineage_depth() lineage_depth(taxon)

Return the number of nodes in a taxon's lineage. Useful for assessing how well a taxon is resolved in The Taxonomicon before running distance computations.

→ integer / int
is_member() is_member(taxon, clade)

Test whether a taxon is a member of a given clade by checking whether the clade name appears in the taxon's lineage.

→ logical / bool
filter_clade() filter_clade(taxa, clade)

Filter a vector of taxon names, retaining only those that belong to a specified clade. Calls is_member() for each taxon.

→ character vector / list of strings
check_coverage() check_coverage(taxa)

Check which taxa in a vector are present in The Taxonomicon. Returns a named logical vector. Use before distance_matrix() on large datasets — taxa returning FALSE will produce NA distances.

→ named logical vector / Series of bool
Multivariate analysis
taxo_cluster() taxo_cluster(taxa, method = "average")

Perform hierarchical clustering (UPGMA by default) on the taxonomic distance matrix. Returns an object containing the hclust result and the distance matrix, which can be converted to a phylo tree via ape::as.phylo().

→ list: hclust, dist_matrix
taxo_ordinate() taxo_ordinate(taxa_or_dist)

Perform principal coordinate analysis (PCoA) on the taxonomic distance matrix. Accepts either a vector of taxon names or a precomputed dist object. Returns coordinates and goodness-of-fit statistics. Compatible with vegan plotting functions.

→ list: points, eigenvalues, gof
taxo_heatmap() taxo_heatmap(taxa_or_dist)

Plot a distance heatmap with marginal dendrograms. Rows and columns are reordered by hierarchical clustering. Accepts either a vector of taxon names or a precomputed dist object.

→ invisibly returns the plot object
Cache management
clear_cache() clear_cache()

Remove all entries from the in-memory lineage cache. Subsequent calls to any function requiring a lineage will trigger fresh network requests.

→ invisibly returns NULL
save_cache() save_cache(path)

Serialise the current in-memory cache to an .rds file (R) or .pkl file (Python). Call at the end of a session to preserve retrieved lineages for future use.

→ invisibly returns the path
load_cache() load_cache(path)

Restore a previously saved cache from disk into the current session. Call before any distance functions to avoid redundant network requests. The loaded cache merges with any existing in-memory cache.

→ invisibly returns NULL
cache_info() cache_info()

Report the current state of the in-memory cache: number of cached lineages, total memory used, and a preview of cached taxon names.

→ prints to console, invisibly returns a list
